programmes.show_description: Alice flushes away Lucy's toilet tatt, Jay tackles Tom's superhero design, Sketch covers Chris's boastful nickname and Glen removes Darren's chat-up line leg art.
There are fires, alarms, bizarre banquets and surprising sound effects, as our five comics complete tasks under the watchful eye of Greg Davies.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo. Alex Horne and The Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
John Hartmann, a former FBI agent and the president and CEO of Chicago-based hardware co-operative True Value, goes incognito among his workforce.
Bill Peduto, the 60th mayor of Pittsburgh, goes incognito among his workforce, to hear from the city's employees and ensure that the changes he's introduced have been effective.
In Cornwall, there are Friesian cows, pitchforks and zombie apocalypse advice, in an eclectic edition of the dining competition.
In Lincolnshire and Yorkshire, there's a one-man comedy show, a posh take on some Yorkshire classics, and a big surprise for one guest.
...Selling: Jeremy and Dayna need some real estate therapy. As the brothers dish out the diagnoses, Dayna realises that even after 10 years, she might not be ready to move.
The One in an Old Mine: It's high noon for impulsive groom Steve as he plans a cliff-hanger of a Wild West wedding for Leona. Will Steve's nuptials on a bridge prove a swinging success?
The One with the Wight Wedding: Groom Liam wants a wedding day that celebrates his homeland, the Isle of Wight. Bride Kandi wants a more standard day. Will it be all-white or go all wrong?
Courtland Gray, chief operating officer of Mississippi-based audio equipment manufacturer Peavey Electronics, goes incognito in his workforce. How do the front-line staff feel?
Todd Pedersen, the founder and CEO of Vivint, a leading solar and home security firm based in Utah, goes undercover among his employees, posing as a reality show contestant.
Giuseppi's: Gordon Ramsay visits a family-owned Italian in Michigan, run by a father and son who constantly clash. Can he get the restaurant - and the family - back on track?
Handlebar: Gordon Ramsay takes on a time warp at the Handlebar restaurant and lounge in Mount Sinai, New York, where the owners are stuck in the 80s with an unenthusiastic chef.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Stuck in real estate limbo, Chandra and Tom have decreased the value of their property. The bros try to raise the bar with an ambitious upgrade.
New Capt, New Rules: Captain Kerry Titheradge takes control of the 197-foot St David as he greets his crew - faces old and new - off the coast of Grenada.
Greg Davies is impressed by Charlotte Ritchie's snack-munching methods and Jamali Maddix's pretty wings, as the race to win Greg's skull-trophy continues.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Richard Ayoade take on Jon Richardson and Katherine Ryan. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Miles Jupp take on Jon Richardson and Sophie Duker. Lee and Dean join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Joe Wilkinson and Rachel Riley.
Conference: At the annual JLB conference in Kettering, Mark's boss, Johnson (Paterson Joseph), tells Mark he has to give a presentation in front of the entire board.
Gym: Mark is still having doubts about marrying Sophie, so joins a gym in order to spend as much time away from her as possible.
Lisa pays tribute to her idol, Jack covers his literal leg tattoo, Alice cleans up Lana's dirty mouth, while Sketch runs rings around Sergiu's romantic gesture.
Greg Davies is impressed by Charlotte Ritchie's snack-munching methods and Jamali Maddix's pretty wings, as the race to win Greg's skull-trophy continues.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Richard Ayoade take on Jon Richardson and Katherine Ryan. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Jose Costa is the president of top automotive care franchise Maaco, based in North Carolina. He dons a disguise to meet the staff who keep his firm on the road to success.
Gina Rivera is the president of Phenix Salon Suites, which rents boutiques to hairdressers. She goes incognito among her franchisees to see how the company could be improved.
In an edition from Portsmouth and the Isle of Wight, Maddi and Givon serve fine champagne and fusion food, Kate and Dan play it safe, and Ali and John's risky menu causes kitchen chaos.
In Worthing and Portsmouth, pensioners Bill and Hilary, dance fans Micaela and Sanchez and kids' entertainer Doug and his partner Patsy compete in a week of hippy happenings and hip-hop.
...Selling: Looks can be deceiving with Anthony and Rachel's home but if you dig a little deeper, the cracks start to show. Can Drew and Jonathan excavate and restore this place?
...in the Snow: Laid-back Liam plans to wow his bride with a wintry white wedding. But 2000 miles away in Portugal, bride-to-be Georgia is dreaming of sun, sand and sangria.
The One with the Thai Stag Do: Cheeky chappie Ian travels to Thailand to get three suits made, picks up a wedding dress for his bride Jay, and has an unforgettable stag do.
Nader Masadeh, the president and CEO of Buffalo Wings & Rings, goes undercover in his own company to ensure that his standards are being kept across the brand.
Shawnon Bellah, chief operating officer of Nestlé Toll House Café by Chip, works undercover among her staff, learning about cookie decoration and the drive-thru experience.
Sabatiello's: Gordon Ramsay sets his sights on Sabatiello's, an Italian restaurant in Connecticut that's better known for its owner Sammy's tantrums than the food.
Trobiano's: Gordon Ramsay meets struggling executive chef Anthony Trobiano, who's sinking under half a million dollars of debt at his restaurant in Great Neck, New York.
and Selling: Brand new series: Can the brothers prescribe the remedy needed to bring nurse Olivia and fireman Dan's current home back to life?
Pier Pressure: Fraser deals with escalating tension. Jared makes a mistake. And Captain Kerry docks St David for the first time, but will it end in disaster?
Mike Wozniak issues a stark warning. Lee Mack has a makeover. And there's a storm brewing on Taskmaster Island...
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges take on Jon Richardson and Victoria Coren Mitchell. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on Jon Richardson and Chris McCausland. Nick Helm is in Dictionary Corner and Joe Wilkinson is at the numbers board.
Handyman: Jeremy meets his hero - techno musician Russell 'The Orgazoid' - and ends up working as his handyman.
Holiday: Jeremy has organised a stag weekend. This consists of Mark and Jeremy on a canal boat on the Shropshire Union Canal. Unfortunately, it's cold and there's nothing to do.
